title: ExpertReport
description: Single expert's output from a preflight panel review. Every role must emit this exact shape.

role.description: Role name matching roles/<name>.md frontmatter, or an ad-hoc name proposed by Selector.

out_of_scope.description: Topics the role explicitly recognized but deferred to another role. Synthesizer consumes this for cross-confirmation weighting and untouched-concerns tracking.

kb_candidates.description: Proposed additions/updates to the role's accumulated KB for this project. Coordinator filters by surviving findings (those that passed the synthesizer noise filter) before applying. Optional: a report with no candidates is fine.

op.description: add = new fact; deprecate = mark existing KB bullet as no longer true; confirm-refresh = re-stamp existing entry with today's SHA.

section.description: Topic heading inside the role's KB file (e.g., 'Auth model', 'Rate limiting', 'Known incidents').

content.description: For 'add': the bullet text with file:line references. For 'deprecate': key phrase identifying the existing entry. For 'confirm-refresh': same as deprecate — key phrase to match.

finding_ref.description: Title or id of the finding in THIS report that justifies the candidate. Coordinator drops candidates whose parent finding was filtered out by the synthesizer.

last_verified_sha.description: Optional: the git SHA at which this fact was verified. Coordinator fills from workspace _index.json if omitted.

adversarial_responses.description: Populated during adversarial round (step 7.5). Absent in first-pass reports and on panels that skip adversarial mode.

target_finding_id.description: Stable id of the peer finding: '<role>:<tier>:<index>'

evidence.description: Required for challenge and refine; empty string for concede/pass.

corrected_replacement.description: Required for refine; empty string otherwise.

evidence.description: Cite artifact line/section OR a concrete scenario. No 'might be' hedging.

evidence_source.description: How the evidence was obtained. code_cited = file:line verified by grep during this run. doc_cited = URL + verbatim quote from an external doc/spec. artifact_self = quote from the reviewed artifact about what the artifact itself proposes/states (internal contradictions, missing steps in a plan, ordering issues) — valid for MUST-FIX. artifact_code_claim = quote from the artifact about how production code allegedly behaves, where you have NOT independently grepped the code — synthesizer auto-downgrades MUST→SHOULD unless cross-confirmed by a code_cited finding from another role. reasoning = expert judgement without external citation; auto-downgraded MUST→SHOULD. Reports from legacy roles without this field are treated as 'reasoning'. Legacy reports using the pre-v0.4.0 'artifact_cited' value are treated as 'artifact_code_claim' (safer default).
